raw_json: {"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"animal_fact","confidence":0.93,"matched_text":"sea turtles do have predators while they're underwater, such as sharks, killer whales, and some species of large predatory fish.","distilled_fact":"Sea turtles have predators while they are underwater, including sharks, killer whales, and some species of large predatory fish."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"animal_fact","confidence":0.9,"matched_text":"sea turtles have evolved several adaptations to help them evade predators while underwater, including their hard shell, camouflage, and the ability to hold their breath for extended periods.","distilled_fact":"Sea turtles have evolved adaptations to evade predators while they are underwater, including their hard shell, camouflage, and the ability to hold their breath for extended periods."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"animal_fact","confidence":0.88,"matched_text":"sea turtles tend to feed on prey that is too small to be of much interest to larger predators, which helps to reduce their risk of predation while they're submerged.","distilled_fact":"Sea turtles tend to feed on prey that is too small to be of much interest to larger predators."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"animal_fact","confidence":0.86,"matched_text":"Despite these adaptations, sea turtles are still vulnerable to predation, especially when nesting or as hatchlings emerging from their nests.","distilled_fact":"Sea turtles are still vulnerable to predation, especially when nesting."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"animal_fact","confidence":0.85,"matched_text":"Despite these adaptations, sea turtles are still vulnerable to predation, especially when nesting or as hatchlings emerging from their nests.","distilled_fact":"Sea turtles are still vulnerable to predation as hatchlings emerging from their nests."}]}
